text: : كُنْتُ عِنْدَ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَسَأَلَهُ رَجُلٌ فَقَالَ لَهُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ إِنَّ اَلشَّمْسَ تَنْقَضُّ ثُمَّ تَرْكُدُ سَاعَةً مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ تَزُولَ فَقَالَ «إِنَّهَا تُؤَامِرُ أَ تَزُولُ أَوْ لاَ تَزُولُ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.677 - It was narrated from Hariz ibn Abdullah that He said: I was with Abu 'Abdillah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) when a man asked him,
"May I be sacrificed for you, the sun seems to descend (move downward) and then becomes still for a moment before it reaches its zenith."
Imam (as) replied: "It consults whether it should continue or not”.
